executiveSummary.text: Risk regime shifted to risk-off/valuation digestion: S&P 500 -0.79%, Nasdaq -1.55%, Dow -0.26%, VIX +14.17% to 17.16. The selloff was not broad panic; it was concentrated in semis/AI hardware plus an oil shock. Probability: 55% choppy consolidation, 30% renewed risk-on if oil cools, 15% deeper de-risking if rates/oil keep rising. Confidence: medium.

Rates/USD are again the valuation governor: 10Y yield 4.61%, 30Y 5.10%, 2Y proxy 3M bill 3.73%, DXY 101.28 (+0.30%). Fresh Fed commentary from Waller warned higher rates may be needed if inflation stays elevated; CME/FedWatch headlines put July hike odds near 46.5%.

Oil is the macro transmission channel: WTI +10.52% to $78.92 and Brent +10.76% to $84.19 after renewed Strait of Hormuz/Iran shipping escalation. Second order: oil raises inflation expectations/term premium; third order: Fed optionality narrows and long-duration equity multiples compress.

AI/semis had an abnormal shakeout: SOXX -4.77%, SMH -4.16%, with KL holdings LRCX -5.83%, AMAT -4.50%, MU -4.32%, KLAC -4.00%, ASML -3.97%, AVGO -3.98%, NVDA -3.52%. Trigger was SK Hynix/memory profit-taking plus higher yields/oil, despite TSMC reporting quarterly sales +36% and June revenue +68%.

Singapore remains a ballast sleeve rather than the problem: STI +0.65% by Yahoo data / Business Times reported nearly flat close at a fresh high; DBS +0.48%, OCBC +0.18%, UOB -0.90%. Banks retain earnings-rate support, while REITs remain capped by global yields.

Commodity/scarcity sleeve split sharply: energy up, metals/miners down. Gold -2.38% futures / GLD -2.62%, silver -3.21% / SLV -3.32%, COPX -2.86%, URNM -5.78%. This is not “geopolitics = gold up”; stronger USD/real-rate fears and liquidity selling dominated haven demand.

regime.label: Risk-off shock, not yet systemic — oil/rates pressure + AI-semiconductor de-rating

usGlobal.what: US equities fell with Nasdaq leadership weakening
usGlobal.why: Higher oil, higher yields, and chip profit-taking hit long-duration/growth multiples; VIX moved up but stayed below panic levels.
usGlobal.implication: Treat as a valuation/liquidity shock unless credit spreads or VIX confirm broader stress.

ratesMacro.why: Inflation remains sticky and Fed Governor Waller reportedly said rates may need to rise if inflation stays elevated; CNBC cited July hike odds rising to 46.5%.
ratesMacro.implication: Long-duration assets need earnings acceleration to offset higher discount rates.

asiaSingapore.what: STI and banks held up near highs
asiaSingapore.why: Local banks benefit from higher-for-longer NIM expectations and defensive dividend demand; Business Times reported DBS crossing S$200B market cap amid earnings optimism.
asiaSingapore.implication: Singapore sleeve is ballast, but do not chase banks aggressively after record-level rerating.

asiaSingapore.why: Global yields rose and USD strengthened; REITs with data-centre or high-quality Singapore office exposure have better demand resilience but funding-cost headwinds remain.
asiaSingapore.implication: Accumulate only on yield-spread widening, not on broad dividend hunger.

commodities.what: Oil spiked; precious metals and miners fell
commodities.why: Hormuz/Iran risk lifted crude, but stronger USD, higher rate expectations, and liquidity selling hit gold/silver/copper miners/uranium ETFs.
commodities.implication: Inflation shock is the key; energy helps producers but hurts consumers and duration.

geopolitics.what: Strait of Hormuz escalation became market-relevant
geopolitics.why: Reuters/CNBC/NYT-style live coverage and Guardian/Al Jazeera headlines describe renewed US-Iran/shipping restrictions; the strait carries a large share of global seaborne oil flows.
geopolitics.implication: Transmission path is shipping/insurance/tanker delays → oil → inflation expectations → yields/Fed → equity multiples.

moves.why: SK Hynix/memory reversal and oil/rate shock triggered profit-taking across AI hardware. KL abnormal movers: LRCX -5.83%, AMAT -4.50%, MU -4.32%, KLAC -4.00%, ASML -3.97%, AVGO -3.98%, NVDA -3.52%; SOXX -4.77%.
moves.implication: Thesis damage is low so far because TSMC sales data still confirm AI demand; valuation/positioning damage is real. 2nd order: equipment capex names de-rate if memory/HBM supply expectations get questioned. 3rd order: if oil sustains, hyperscaler cost of capital rises and AI capex duration gets scrutinized.
moves.action: HOLD / WATCH. Add only in staged tranches if SOXX falls another 5-8% without negative order-book evidence; invalidator is hyperscaler capex cuts or foundry/equipment guidance deterioration.

moves.what: QQQ and GRID breached ETF move thresholds
moves.why: Nasdaq/growth de-rating and rate sensitivity: QQQ -1.90%, GRID -1.86%.
moves.implication: GRID remains core to AI-power bottleneck thesis but is duration-sensitive; QQQ weakness is broad multiple compression rather than single-name thesis break.
moves.action: HOLD. Add GRID only on rate-driven weakness if power-equipment orders/backlogs remain firm; invalidator is grid-capex cancellation or margin collapse.

moves.what: Scarcity-metals sleeve had liquidation, not haven behavior
moves.why: GLD -2.62%, SLV -3.32%, COPX -2.86%, URNM -5.78% despite oil/geopolitical stress, as USD/rates/liquidity dominated.
moves.implication: This confirms KL should not expect every scarcity asset to hedge every shock. 2nd order: higher energy costs can pressure miners margins; 3rd order: sustained inflation can eventually support hard assets only if real yields/trust regime turn favorable.
moves.action: HOLD / WATCH. Do not chase; add only when real yields stop rising or copper/uranium physical tightness reasserts. Invalidate near-term long if USD and yields keep breaking higher.

moves.why: DBS +0.48% to S$70.79, OCBC +0.18%, UOB -0.90%; Business Times reported DBS crossed S$200B market cap and banks near records.
moves.implication: Dividend ballast is working, but forward returns are more valuation-dependent after the rally.
moves.action: HOLD / TRIM only if allocation drifts too high; avoid fresh chase unless valuations reset.

thesisImpact.what: AI scarcity thesis intact but crowded
thesisImpact.why: TSMC quarterly sales +36% and June revenue +68% support end-demand; memory/semicap selloff shows positioning is vulnerable.
thesisImpact.implication: Distinguish demand signal from price action: keep core, demand better entry prices.
thesisImpact.action: HOLD; staged ADD on confirmed demand + lower valuation.

thesisImpact.why: Cash at ~4%, CPF, low-rate mortgage and Singapore dividends dampen forced-selling risk while growth/commodity sleeves absorb volatility.
thesisImpact.implication: KL can use volatility opportunistically without needing to predict the exact bottom.
thesisImpact.action: Keep dry powder; no all-in risk.

narratives.title: Oil shock collides with AI multiple expansion
narratives.description: The market was pricing AI growth plus benign liquidity; a Hormuz-linked crude spike forces investors to re-price inflation, term premium and Fed reaction function.
narratives.source: Brave last-24h: CNBC/NYT/Guardian/Al Jazeera/Investopedia oil-Hormuz coverage; Yahoo market data
narratives.priceImpact: Negative for duration/growth; positive for energy cash flows.

narratives.title: AI demand data positive; AI stock positioning negative
narratives.description: TSMC sales +36% and June revenue +68% indicate demand strength, but SK Hynix/memory profit-taking hit the semiconductor complex.
narratives.priceImpact: Short-term negative; long-term neutral-positive if capex guidance holds.

narratives.title: Singapore bank rally as defensive yield plus higher-rate beneficiary
narratives.description: DBS crossed S$200B market cap per Business Times; STI near/fresh highs while broader global risk softened.
narratives.source: Business Times via Brave, 13 hours ago; Yahoo prices

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil spike → inflation expectations and term premium → Fed less able to ease / possible hike risk → higher discount rates → AI/growth multiple compression even if earnings remain strong.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Do not confuse good AI fundamentals with immunity to macro discount-rate shocks.

secondOrderEffects.text: Higher energy prices → miner input costs rise → copper/silver/uranium equities can fall even when scarcity narratives are intact.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Physical scarcity and miner ETF returns can diverge during liquidity/rate shocks.

secondOrderEffects.text: Singapore banks benefit from higher rates initially, but too much global rate stress eventually hurts credit growth, asset quality and REIT collateral values.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Bank sleeve is ballast, not a one-way bet.

secondOrderEffects.text: If Hormuz risk persists, Asian importers face worse terms of trade; SGD may be buffered by MAS credibility but REITs and consumers still face funding/cost pressure.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Watch SGD, oil and S-REIT yield spreads together.

investment.thesis: Keep AI scarcity core, but require evidence-based adds after shakeouts.
investment.trigger: Add 10-20% of intended tranche if SOXX/SMH fall another 5-8% while TSMC/equipment/hyperscaler capex guidance remains intact.
investment.whyNow: Crowding is being flushed while fundamental TSMC sales data remains strong.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, TSMC guide-down, or equipment order cancellations.

investment.thesis: Use Singapore income sleeve as ballast, not chase.
investment.trigger: Add REITs only when yield spreads widen meaningfully and 10Y yield stops rising; add banks only on valuation reset or earnings upside.
investment.whyNow: Banks are near record/rich levels; REITs are rate-capped but selective assets have demand resilience.
investment.invalidator: Credit deterioration or persistent global yield breakout.

trading.thesis: Oil shock fade is tradable only if shipping risk de-escalates.
trading.trigger: Brent reverses below $80 with lower war-risk headlines and VIX below 16.
trading.whyNow: Current move is geopolitical premium-heavy; reversal would relieve inflation/rate pressure.
trading.invalidator: Sustained Hormuz disruption or Brent above $90.

trading.thesis: Avoid catching falling semiconductor knives intraday.
trading.trigger: Wait for SOXX/SMH to stabilize above prior session low with improving breadth; prefer leaders with fundamental news support.
trading.whyNow: Forced/profit-taking can overshoot even with intact thesis.

watchlist.trigger: 10Y above 4.75% or 30Y above 5.20% with DXY rising.
watchlist.whyNow: This would pressure all long-duration sleeves and REITs.
watchlist.invalidator: Inflation data softens and yields reverse below 4.45% on 10Y.

watchlist.thesis: AI capex confirmation matters more than daily price action.
watchlist.trigger: TSMC earnings/guidance, hyperscaler capex commentary, ASML/LRCX/AMAT order data.
watchlist.whyNow: Price action says crowding; revenue data says demand. Need guidance to resolve.
watchlist.invalidator: Order pushouts, inventory build, or HBM oversupply signs.

riskWatch.name: Oil/Hormuz inflation shock
riskWatch.description: Brent +10.76% makes the geopolitical risk macro-relevant via inflation expectations, shipping insurance and Fed reaction function.
riskWatch.mitigation: Do not increase duration aggressively; keep cash dry powder and watch Brent $90 / de-escalation below $80.

riskWatch.name: AI/semiconductor crowding unwind
riskWatch.description: SOXX -4.77% and multiple KL holdings down >3% show crowded positioning can overwhelm good demand headlines.

riskWatch.name: Rates and USD squeeze
riskWatch.description: 10Y 4.61%, 30Y 5.10%, DXY 101.28 pressure growth, REITs and non-USD risk assets.
riskWatch.mitigation: Favor balance-sheet quality, avoid leverage, use cash buffer.

keyEvents.text: CNBC/Investopedia/Yahoo: US stocks ended lower; Nasdaq led declines as chips fell and oil surged on Hormuz escalation.
Bloomberg/CNBC/Yahoo: TSMC reported quarterly sales +36%; June revenue reportedly +68%, reinforcing AI chip demand.
Business Times: Singapore shares near flat/fresh high; DBS crossed S$200B market cap amid bank rally.
NYT/CNBC: Fed Governor Waller warned rates may need to rise if inflation stays elevated; rate-hike odds rose in FedWatch headlines.

bottomLine.whatMatters: The market is digesting an oil/rates shock on top of crowded AI positioning. The key variables are Brent, 10Y/30Y yields, DXY and semiconductor guidance — not one-day price noise alone.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold core AI scarcity positions but add only in tranches after confirmation; keep cash ballast; avoid chasing Singapore banks after record-level rerating; use REIT weakness only when yield pressure stabilizes.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Ignore simplistic “geopolitics means gold up” and “AI is broken” narratives. Gold fell because USD/rates/liquidity dominated; AI demand remains supported by TSMC data unless guidance says otherwise.
